title: Undefined value in Gmail node due to data flow issue
symptom: The Gmail node does not receive the expected data, resulting in an undefined value being sent. This occurs when the data is not properly passed through the workflow.
applies_when: Data is generated in a different branch
not_this_if: Data is correctly passed in the same branch
No undefined values in the workflow
root_cause: The issue arises when the data required by the Gmail node is created in a different execution path than the one it is trying to access. If nodes are not properly connected or merged, the necessary data may not be available, leading to undefined values.
fix_steps: Ensure nodes 'Setup1' and 'Normalize company name' exist with the correct names.
Verify that the sequence 'Normalize company name > Get latest news > Edit Fields > Gmail' is in the same branch.
Add a Merge node before 'Edit Fields' to combine 'companyName' and 'articles'.
In the 'Edit Fields' node, set 'Latest news' as a fixed field name and place the expression in the value field.
agent_instruction: Check node names, ensure proper data flow, and adjust the Edit Fields node accordingly.
